all_retrieved_content.page_content: In September 2021, Lotus Technology Group’s subsidiary, WFOE, entered into an exchangeable note agreement with an investor. Pursuant to the agreement, WFOE is entitled to issue, from time to time, exchangeable notes (the “WFOE Exchangeable Notes”) to obtain financing from the investor (the “WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder”) up to RMB3,000,000 with a coupon rate of 3% per annum. Each tranche of WFOE Exchangeable Notes is scheduled to mature on the one-year anniversary date of issuance. The repayments of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes were guaranteed by Founders Onshore Vehicle. Lotus Technology Group issued RMB800,000 (equivalent to US$125,039), RMB600,000 (equivalent to US$94,130), RMB600,000 (equivalent to US$94,457), RMB800,000 (equivalent to US$118,585), and RMB200,000 (equivalent to US$27,883) on November 5, 2021, January 7, 2022, January 18, 2022, August 2, 2022, and September 22, 2023, to the WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder, respectively. Among these, RMB400,000 issued in November 2021 was exchanged for Series A redeemable convertible preferred shares in December 2022, as mentioned in note 17. At the time when there is a subsequent round of equity financing, upon the notification in writing by Lotus Technology Group, the WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder is entitled to convert the whole or any portion of the outstanding principal amount of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes into the shares of the subsequent round of equity financing at the post-money equity valuation based on a fixed monetary amount.

The fair value of the guarantees and Rights and Obligations provided by the Founders Onshore Vehicle was US$909 for the tranche issued in September 2023, US$8,135 for the tranches issued during the year ended December 31, 2022, and US$3,391 for the tranche issued in November 2021. The fair value of the guarantees and Rights and Obligations was treated as debt issuance cost and charged to the interest expenses in the consolidated and combined statements of comprehensive loss. On April 27, 2023, Lotus Technology Group entered into a subscription agreement with the WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder (the “Jingkai Subscription Agreement”), pursuant to which the WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder has committed to subscribe for and purchase Lotus Technology Group’s ordinary shares for US\$10.00 per share, for an investment amount of RMB2,600,000 (equivalent to US\$367,092), which will be settled by the then outstanding principal amount of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the Jingkai Subscription Agreement, the obligations of the parties to consummate the investment are subject to the satisfaction or waiver of certain customary closing conditions of the respective parties, including, among others, (a) all conditions precedent under the Merger Agreement having been satisfied or waived, and (b) all of the consents from, and filings and/or registrations with, applicable governmental authority as required by applicable PRC laws for the WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder’s outbound direct investment in Lotus Technology Group shall have been duly obtained and completed (collectively as “Closing Conditions”). The Jingkai Subscription Agreement shall terminate upon the earlier to occur of (a) termination of the Merger Agreement, and (b) March 15, 2024, or such later termination date of the Merger Agreement as agreed among the parties thereto, among other things. Pursuant to the above agreements, the maturity dates of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes were extended to the earlier of March 15, 2024, and termination of the Merger Agreement. Lotus Technology Group evaluated the amendment in accordance with ASC 470 - 50, Modifications and Extinguishments, and concluded that the change in present values of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes before and after the amendment was less than 10%. Therefore, Lotus Technology Group determined the amendment did not represent an extinguishment. The fair value change arising from the modification of US$661 was reflected in the fair values of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes. Lotus Technology Group elected the fair value option to account for the WFOE Exchangeable Notes, including the component related to accrued interest.

title: LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
 
summary: ### Summary of Business and Organizational Overview

Lotus Technology Inc. operates in the automotive and technology sectors, focusing on electric and autonomous vehicles. The company emphasizes innovation, sustainability, and advanced engineering, aiming to enhance user experience through smart connectivity and intelligent systems. Its organizational structure includes a significant number of ordinary shares, with a notable portion held by the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP and public shareholders. The company has undergone various mergers and acquisitions, including a reorganization to streamline operations and enhance control over its subsidiaries.

As of February 23, 2024, Lotus Technology's ordinary shares and Public Warrants began trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW." The company has issued multiple types of financial instruments, including Public Warrants, Sponsor Warrants, and exchangeable notes, which have been categorized based on their fair value measurements. The financial statements reflect a continuation of operations post-merger, treating the mergers as recapitalizations without recognizing goodwill or intangible assets.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Group believes the fair value option best reflects the economics of the underlying transaction. The WFOE Exchangeable Notes were recognized at fair value at the issuance date and are measured subsequently at fair value. The changes in fair values of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes due to the instrument-specific credit risk of US$272, US$33, and US$132 were debited/(credited) to other comprehensive income (loss), and all other changes in fair values of US$737, US$13,162, and US$1,065 were recognized as “Changes in fair values of mandatorily redeemable noncontrolling interest, exchangeable notes and convertible notes, excluding impact of instrument-specific credit risk” in the consolidated and combined statements of comprehensive loss for the years ended December 31, 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. Lotus Technology Group adopted a scenario-weighted average method to estimate the fair value of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes, based on an analysis of future values of the settlement of the obligation, assuming various outcomes. The probability weightings assigned to certain potential scenarios were based on management’s assessment of the probability of settlement of the liability in cash or shares and an assessment of the timing of settlement. In each scenario, the obligation valuation was based on the contractually agreed cash payment or equivalent equity discounted to each valuation date. The fair values of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes are estimated with the following key assumptions used:

- **Table Title**: Key Assumptions for Valuation of WFOE Exchangeable Notes  
- **Table Summary**: This table presents the key financial and probabilistic assumptions used to estimate the fair value of WFOE Exchangeable Notes as of December 31, 2023, compared to December 31, 2022. The assumptions include risk-free interest rates, discount rates, probabilities related to conversion and qualified IPOs, and bond yield ranges.  
- **Context**: The valuation methodology leverages a scenario-weighted average approach, incorporating varying outcomes for settlement (cash or shares) and timing. Each scenario's valuation was derived from cash payment or equity equivalents, discounted appropriately.

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1**: The risk-free interest rate as of December 31, 2023, is 1.75%, compared to 2.07% as of December 31, 2022.  
- **Row 2**: The discount rate remains consistent at 20.00% for both December 31, 2023, and December 31, 2022.  
- **Row 3**: The probability of conversion increased significantly in 2023, reaching 90.00%, compared to a range of 50.00%–75.00% in 2022.  
- **Row 4**: Bond yields for 2023 are in the range of 3.27%–4.06%, which is substantially lower than the 7.35%–7.76% range recorded for 2022.  
- **Row 5**: The probability of occurrence of a Qualified IPO climbed from 45.00% in 2022 to 90.00% in 2023.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes In November 2022, Lotus Technology Group’s subsidiary, Hangzhou Lightning Speed Technology Co., Ltd. (“Lightning Speed”), entered into an exchangeable note agreement with an investor. Pursuant to the agreement, Lightning Speed is entitled to issue exchangeable notes (the “Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes”) of RMB1,000,000 to obtain financing from the investor (the “Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes Holder”). The repayments of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes were guaranteed by the immediate shareholders of Lightning Speed, i.e., Sanya Lotus Venture Investment Limited Company (“Sanya Lotus”), a wholly owned subsidiary of the VIE, and Ningbo Weili Enterprise Management Consulting Partnership (Limited Partnership). The WFOE provides a joint and several liability guarantee for the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes. In December 2022, Hangzhou Lightning Speed Technology Co., Ltd. issued the first tranche of RMB500,000 (equivalent to US$71,792) to the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes Holder. Each tranche of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes is scheduled to mature on the five-year anniversary date of issuance and bears a simple interest rate of the loan prime rate published by the China Foreign Exchange Trade System (“LPR”). With the consent of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes Holder, Hangzhou Lightning Speed Technology Co., Ltd. can extend the term of each tranche of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes twice, but each extension shall not exceed one year. During the extension period, the interest on the outstanding principal of the Lightning Speed Exchangeable Notes is calculated at the simple interest rate of 110% of LPR. All interest shall be paid at maturity.

summary: ### Summary of Business and Organizational Overview

Lotus Technology Inc. operates in the automotive and technology sectors, focusing on electric and autonomous vehicles. The company emphasizes innovation, sustainability, and advanced engineering, aiming to enhance user experience through smart connectivity and intelligent systems. Its organizational structure includes a significant number of ordinary shares, with a notable portion held by the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP and public shareholders. The company has undergone various mergers and acquisitions, including a reorganization to streamline operations and enhance control over its subsidiaries.

As of February 23, 2024, Lotus Technology's ordinary shares and Public Warrants began trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW." The company has issued multiple types of financial instruments, including Public Warrants, Sponsor Warrants, and exchangeable notes, which have been categorized based on their fair value measurements. The financial statements reflect a continuation of operations post-merger, treating the mergers as recapitalizations without recognizing goodwill or intangible assets.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: ● If there is any occurrence of events of default, the Subsidiary Convertible Note Holder has the right to require the immediate parent company of Ningbo Robotics and the Variable Interest Entity (VIE) to redeem all outstanding principal amounts of the Subsidiary Convertible Note and settle the unpaid interest immediately. The redemption price upon the occurrence of events of default equals (i) the outstanding principal, plus (ii) the outstanding principal × interest rate ÷ 365 × the number of days from the issuance date to the redemption date, less (iii) the interest already paid for the outstanding principal. If the immediate parent company of Ningbo Robotics or the VIE delays in payments, a 0.05% penalty per day of the overdue redemption price shall be paid to the Subsidiary Convertible Note Holder. During the years ended December 31, 2023, and 2022, no default events occurred. Lotus Technology and the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE) provide a joint and several liability guarantee for the Subsidiary Convertible Note. Lotus Technology elected the fair value option for the Subsidiary Convertible Note, including the component related to accrued interest. Lotus Technology believes the fair value option best reflects the economics of the underlying transaction. The Subsidiary Convertible Note was recognized at fair value at the issuance date and is measured subsequently at fair value.

It highlights changes in subsidy amounts and their cumulative totals.  
- **Context**: The table analyzes subsidies related to asset construction and research and development (R&D) activities for Lotus Technology. The subsidies reflect government grants received and income recognition, alongside adjustments for currency fluctuations.  
- **Special Notes**: All amounts are stated in thousands of US dollars (US$). Values include adjustments for foreign currency translation. Totals are calculated by summing the asset-related and R&D-related subsidies for each category.  

[Row Level]  

- **Row 1**: As of January 1, 2021, the balance was US$541,592, entirely related to R&D-related subsidies, as no asset-related subsidies were reported. The total subsidy balance at this date was US$541,592.  
- **Row 2**: During 2021, government grants of US$279,052 were received for asset-related subsidies, while no additional grants were received for R&D-related subsidies.  
- **Row 3**: Income of US$490,461 was recognized during 2021, all attributed to R&D-related subsidies; no amount was recognized for asset-related subsidies during this period.  
- **Row 4**: Foreign currency translation adjustments during 2021 resulted in an increase of US$3,270 for asset-related subsidies and US$6,843 for R&D-related subsidies, totaling US$10,113.  
- **Row 5**: By December 31, 2021, the cumulative balances were US$282,322 for asset-related subsidies and US$57,974 for R&D-related subsidies, bringing the total to US$340,296.  
- **Row 6**: In 2022, there were no newly received government grants. Income recognized during this year amounted to US$54,954 for R&D-related subsidies, with no asset-related income reported.  
- **Row 7**: Foreign currency translation adjustments in 2022 led to a decrease of US$23,872 for asset-related subsidies and US$3,020 for R&D-related subsidies, totaling a negative adjustment of US$26,892.  
- **Row 8**: As of December 31, 2022, the balances were US$258,450 for asset-related subsidies and no remaining R&D-related subsidy balance, resulting in a total of US$258,450.  
- **Row 9**: During 2023, government grants amounting to US$16,345 were received exclusively for asset-related subsidies. No grants were received for R&D-related subsidies.  
- **Row 10**: A foreign currency translation adjustment of US$-4,698 was recorded in 2023, affecting only asset-related subsidies.  
- **Row 11**: By December 31, 2023, the balance for asset-related subsidies was US$270,097, while there was no balance for R&D-related subsidies. The total subsidy balance at the end of 2023 stood at US$270,097.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: During the years ended December 31, 2023, 2022, and 2021, Lotus Technology received specific subsidies of US$16,345, nil, and US$279,052 for compensating the expenditures on the construction of Lotus Technology’s corporate buildings and plant (“asset-related subsidy”), respectively. Since the corporate buildings and plant are still under construction as of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology has not recognized any government grants relating to these subsidies in profit or loss. In 2018, Lotus Technology received a specific subsidy of US$755,581 relating to the company's R&D expenditures. During the years ended December 31, 2023, 2022, and 2021, Lotus Technology recognized government grants of nil, US$54,954, and US$490,461 for the R&D expenses incurred under this subsidy, respectively. Lotus Technology received government grants of US$4,077, US$870, and US$233 with no future related costs required during the years ended December 31, 2023, 2022, and 2021, which were directly recognized as government grants in the consolidated and combined statements of comprehensive loss for the years ended December 31, 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. On October 11, 2022, Lotus Technology and holders of Series Pre-A Preferred Shares agreed to extend the redemption date of the Series Pre-A Preferred Shares from February 28, 2027, to October 11, 2027. Lotus Technology determines that the modification to the terms of Series Pre-A Preferred Shares represents a modification, because the difference of the fair values of the redeemable convertible preferred shares immediately before and after the amendment is less than 10%.

- **Table Summary**: This table outlines the key financial and valuation metrics for the redeemable convertible Series Pre-A Preferred Shares both before and after the modification on October 11, 2022. It compares factors such as the risk-free interest rate, expected volatility, dividend yield, expected terms, and the fair value of ordinary shares.  
- **Context**: The modification extended the redemption date of the Series Pre-A Preferred Shares and was determined to have no significant change in fair value, triggering a modification accounting approach. - **Row 2**: The expected volatility decreased from 61.52% before the modification to 60.26% after the modification, indicating a marginal reduction in anticipated share price fluctuations based on historical data of comparable companies.  
- **Row 3**: The expected dividend yield remained constant at 0.00% both before and after the modification, as Lotus Technology does not plan to pay dividends in the near future.  
- **Row 4**: The expected terms of the redeemable convertible preferred shares were updated from a range of 2.22–4.39 years before the modification to 2.22–5.00 years after the modification, reflecting the extension of the redemption date.

Its organizational structure includes a significant number of ordinary shares, with a notable portion held by the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP and public shareholders. The company has undergone various mergers and acquisitions, including a reorganization to streamline operations and enhance control over its subsidiaries.

As of February 23, 2024, Lotus Technology's ordinary shares and Public Warrants began trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW." The company has issued multiple types of financial instruments, including Public Warrants, Sponsor Warrants, and exchangeable notes, which have been categorized based on their fair value measurements. The financial statements reflect a continuation of operations post-merger, treating the mergers as recapitalizations without recognizing goodwill or intangible assets.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Performance Adjustment Mechanism In the event that the Group fails to achieve the agreed milestones, including mass production of the Eletre SUV no later than June 30, 2023, and mass production of the Emeya (Type 133) no later than June 30, 2024 (collectively referred to as “SOP Milestones”) as requested by the Pre-A Investor A, for every six (6) months that the Group is delayed in achieving the SOP Milestones, Lotus Technology shall issue to the Pre-A Investor A an additional number of Series Pre-A Preferred Shares at nominal price (the total consideration shall be RMB 1) that equals 0.5% of the then issued and outstanding shares of Lotus Technology immediately prior to such issuance (on a fully-diluted basis), provided that the aggregate maximum number of Series Pre-A Preferred Shares to be issued and obtained by the Pre-A Investor A shall be no more than 2% of the Series Pre-A Preferred Shares then held by the Pre-A Investor A acquired under the Series Pre-A Preferred Share purchase agreement (collectively referred to as “warrant”). Accounting of Redeemable Convertible Preferred Shares Lotus Technology has classified the redeemable convertible preferred shares as mezzanine equity in the consolidated balance sheets as they are contingently redeemable upon the occurrence of certain events outside of Lotus Technology’s control.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology concluded the embedded conversion and redemption option did not need to be bifurcated pursuant to ASC 815 because these terms do not permit net settlement, nor can they be readily settled net by a means outside the contract, nor can they provide for delivery of an asset that puts the holders in a position not substantially different from net settlement. Lotus Technology also determined that the warrant granted to Pre-A Investor A is a freestanding instrument that was accounted for as a warrant liability, as the warrant could be legally detachable and separately exercisable from the Series Pre-A Preferred Shares. At initial recognition, proceeds were first allocated to the warrant based on its fair value and then the residual to the Series Pre-A Preferred Shares. For subsequent measurement, Lotus Technology recorded the changes in fair value of the warrant liability in the consolidated and combined statements of comprehensive loss. Lotus Technology expects that the agreed SOP Milestones will be achieved, thus Lotus Technology concludes that the fair value of the warrant liability is minimal as of the issuance date and December 31, 2023 and 2022, respectively. The SOP Milestones for the Eletre SUV and the Emeya (Type 133) were achieved on December 31, 2022 and in January 2024, respectively, therefore no additional Series Pre-A Preferred Shares shall be issued. Since Lotus Technology has adopted ASU 2020-06 on January 1, 2021, Lotus Technology did not assess whether the redeemable convertible preferred shares contain beneficial conversion features.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology recognized changes in the redemption value immediately as they occur and adjusts the carrying value of redeemable convertible preferred shares to their maximum redemption amount at the end of each reporting period, as if it were also the redemption date for redeemable convertible preferred shares. As mentioned above, the Series A Preferred Shares issued to the WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder were exchanged by the WFOE Exchangeable Notes issued in November 2021. Lotus Technology evaluated the accounting of the exchange under ASC 470-50 and determined that the exchange qualified for extinguishment accounting. As such, Lotus Technology derecognized the carrying amount of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes of US$60,419 and recognized Series A Preferred Shares of US$60,419. Upon exchange, the accumulated recognized other comprehensive loss relating to fair value changes of the WFOE Exchangeable Notes due to instrument-specific credit risk of US$64 was recycled to the consolidated and combined statements of comprehensive loss. During the year ended December 31, 2022, Lotus Technology did not record any increase in the carrying amount of Series A Preferred Shares issued to the WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holder as the redemption value was lower than the initial carrying amount. As Lotus Technology’s redeemable convertible preferred shares are denominated in RMB other than the functional currency of Lotus Technology, i.e., US$, the measurement of redemption value incorporates the effect of exchange rates on the functional currency amount of the redemption feature.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: On November 12, 2021, Lotus Technology's variable interest entity (VIE) and Momenta (Suzhou) Technology Limited Company (“Momenta”) incorporated Ningbo Robotics. The VIE and Momenta hold 60% and 40% equity interest and invested US$9,449 and US$6,299 in Ningbo Robotics, respectively. Pursuant to the shareholder agreement entered into by Ningbo Robotics and Momenta: 1) If there is any disagreement or disputes arising between Ningbo Robotics and Momenta, Momenta has the right to require the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE) or the Variable Interest Entity (VIE) or the entity designated by WFOE or VIE to acquire the 40% equity interest in Ningbo Robotics at the consideration of RMB40,000 in cash. 2) Momenta is required to sell its 40% equity interest in Ningbo Robotics to WFOE or VIE or the entity designated by WFOE or VIE no later than the third anniversary date of the incorporation of Ningbo Robotics. The redemption price between the date of incorporation and the first anniversary of Ningbo Robotics is RMB40,000, between the first anniversary and the second anniversary of the incorporation of Ningbo Robotics is RMB80,000, and between the second anniversary and the third anniversary of the incorporation of Ningbo Robotics is RMB120,000. At the sole discretion of Momenta, Momenta is entitled to elect either to redeem in cash or exchange for Lotus Technology’s shares with the equivalent monetary value.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: 3) If Lotus Technology’s Board of Directors approved Lotus Technology’s Qualified IPO within the third anniversary date of the incorporation of Ningbo Robotics, Momenta is required to sell its 40% equity interest in Ningbo Robotics at the consideration same as the mechanism mentioned in 2). At the sole discretion of Momenta, Momenta is entitled to elect either to redeem in cash or exchange for Lotus Technology’s shares with the equivalent monetary value. In March 2022, the Variable Interest Entity (VIE) transferred its 60% equity interest in Ningbo Robotics to its wholly owned subsidiary, Sanya Lotus. Lotus Technology is contractually obligated to repurchase the 40% noncontrolling interests (“NCI”) held by Momenta within three years from the incorporation of Ningbo Robotics. The NCI, together with the embedded repurchase contract, is accounted for as a liability and recorded as “Mandatorily redeemable noncontrolling interest” in Lotus Technology’s consolidated balance sheets. On April 28, 2023, Lotus Technology and Momenta entered into an agreement, pursuant to which, Lotus Technology redeemed Momenta’s 40% equity interest in Ningbo Robotics for a cash consideration of RMB80,000 (equivalent to US$11,554) pursuant to Momenta’s redemption right under the shareholders’ agreement of Ningbo Robotics (the “Robotics Redemption”). As mentioned in note 15, on May 30, 2023, Lotus Technology issued a convertible note to Momenta Global Limited, a related party of Momenta, following the Robotics Redemption. Lotus Technology elected the fair value option to account for the mandatorily redeemable noncontrolling interest. Lotus Technology believes the fair value option best reflects the economics of the underlying transaction.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: As noted in Note 1(b), following the Merger Transaction, Lotus Technology’s Articles were amended. As such, the shares and corresponding capital amounts and loss per share prior to the Merger have been retroactively adjusted. The new authorized shares of Lotus Technology are US$50 divided into 5,000,000,000 shares comprising ordinary shares with par value of US$0.00001 each. The number of shares issued and outstanding as of December 31, 2023, and 2022 is 474,621,603 ordinary shares. Under the EIT Law and its implementation rules, an enterprise established outside the PRC with a “place of effective management” within the PRC is considered a PRC resident enterprise for PRC enterprise income tax purposes. A PRC resident enterprise is generally subject to certain PRC tax reporting obligations and a uniform 25% enterprise income tax rate on its worldwide income. The implementation rules to the EIT Law provide that non-resident legal entities are considered PRC residents if substantial and overall management and control over the manufacturing and business operations, personnel, accounting, properties, etc., occurs within the PRC. Despite the present uncertainties resulting from the limited PRC tax guidance on the issue, Lotus Technology does not believe that the legal entities organized outside the PRC should be treated as residents for EIT Law purposes. If the PRC tax authorities subsequently determine that Lotus Technology and its subsidiaries registered outside the PRC are deemed resident enterprises, Lotus Technology and its subsidiaries registered outside the PRC will be subject to the PRC income tax at a rate of 25%.

- **Table Summary**: This table outlines the components contributing to the actual income tax expense for Lotus Technology Inc. for the years 2023, 2022, and 2021. The table compares the adjustments from the computed statutory tax rate impact to the final tax expense figures, providing details about the effects of varying jurisdictions, preferential rates, and adjustments like valuation allowances and tax differences.  
- **Context**: The surrounding text explains that the table illustrates deviations from the computed tax using the PRC statutory tax rate (25%), with specific factors contributing to these differences. It is part of the financial statement notes and includes amounts in thousands of US dollars.  
- **Special Notes**: All figures are reported in thousands of US dollars (US$). Special formatting highlights the "Actual income tax expense" as the final calculated value in bold, spanning across all three years.  

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1**: In 2023, the computed expected tax benefit at the PRC statutory tax rate of 25% was -187,286 US$, compared to -181,068 US$ in 2022 and -27,170 US$ in 2021, representing the baseline computation before any adjustments.  
- **Row 2**: The effect on tax rates in different jurisdictions adjusted the computed tax by 824 US$ in 2023, 5,997 US$ in 2022, and -373 US$ in 2021, reflecting variations due to tax regulations across locations.  
- **Row 3**: Preferential tax rate impacts reduced the computed tax by -423 US$ in 2023, with no adjustments noted for 2022 and 2021.  
- **Row 4**: Non-deductible expenses resulted in a tax effect of 2,474 US$ in 2023, 480 US$ in 2022, and 110 US$ in 2021, increasing the tax expense due to disallowed deductions.  
- **Row 5**: Additional deductions for R&D expenses caused a tax effect of -17,732 US$ in 2023, -3,068 US$ in 2022, and -134 US$ in 2021, reducing the tax burden thanks to these favorable provisions.  
- **Row 6**: A change in the valuation allowance increased the tax expense by 202,586 US$ in 2023, 177,715 US$ in 2022, and 29,784 US$ in 2021, demonstrating significant adjustments in expectations for deferred tax asset utilization.  
- **Row 7**: Tax filing differences added 663 US$ in 2023, with no adjustments recorded for 2022 or 2021 (indicating tax reporting or timing adjustments).  
- **Row 8**: Other factors contributed 7 US$ in 2023, 236 US$ in 2022, and -364 US$ in 2021, encompassing miscellaneous adjustments.  
- **Row 9**: The final "Actual income tax expense" was determined to be 1,113 US$ in 2023, 292 US$ in 2022, and 1,853 US$ in 2021. These are the reported tax expenses after all adjustments.

Its organizational structure includes a significant number of ordinary shares, with a notable portion held by the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP and public shareholders. The company has undergone various mergers and acquisitions, including a reorganization to streamline operations and enhance control over its subsidiaries.

As of February 23, 2024, Lotus Technology's ordinary shares and Public Warrants began trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW." The company has issued multiple types of financial instruments, including Public Warrants, Sponsor Warrants, and exchangeable notes, which have been categorized based on their fair value measurements. The financial statements reflect a continuation of operations post-merger, treating the mergers as recapitalizations without recognizing goodwill or intangible assets.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Valuation Techniques Derivative asset: The derivative asset represents forward currency contracts. The fair value is estimated by discounting the difference between the contractual forward price and the current available forward price for the residual maturity of the contract using observable market rates. Investment securities: On May 13, 2022, Lotus Technology Inc. purchased a one-year convertible note (the “Note”) with the principal of US$10,000 issued by ECARX Holdings Inc. (“Ecarx”), which is a related party controlled by the Controlling Shareholder of Lotus Technology Inc. and listed on NASDAQ on December 21, 2022. Upon the listing of Ecarx, the Note was automatically converted to the Class A ordinary shares of Ecarx (the “listed equity securities”) at a conversion price of US$9.5 per share. Lotus Technology Inc. classifies the listed equity securities as investments with readily determinable fair values and reports them at fair value. As such, Lotus Technology Inc. derecognized the carrying amount of the Note of US$11,316 and recognized the listed equity securities of US$11,316 at fair value on December 21, 2022. Lotus Technology Inc. values the listed equity securities using quoted prices for the underlying securities in active markets and accordingly, Lotus Technology Inc. classifies the valuation techniques that use these inputs as Level 1. As of December 31, 2023 and 2022, Lotus Technology Inc.’s equity securities were US$3,326 and US$8,411, respectively.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: (vi) On March 12, 2021, Lotus Technology entered into a license agreement with Zhejiang Liankong, a subsidiary of Geely Holding. Under the terms of the agreement, Lotus Technology received a non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, and non-sublicensable license for the electric automotive chassis and autonomous driving technology platform (the “Geely License”) with consideration of US$288,948. The Geely License is contractually restricted to be used in certain R&D projects of Lotus Technology. Lotus Technology concludes that the Geely License has no alternative future use; therefore, the cost of the license has been expensed as research and development expenses in the consolidated and combined statement of comprehensive loss for the year ended December 31, 2021. (vii) In November 2021, Lotus Technology obtained the trademark licenses (Note 7) from Group Lotus Limited, a related party controlled by Geely Holding, by issuing Lotus Technology’s 47,995,443 ordinary shares to LGIL, a related party controlled by Geely Holding. The transaction was accounted for as a common control transaction and completed in June 2022. (ix) On May 13, 2022, Lotus Technology purchased a one-year convertible note (the “Note”) with the principal of US$10,000 issued by Ecarx.

title: LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
 
summary: ### Summary of Business and Organizational Overview

Lotus Technology Inc. operates in the automotive and technology sectors, focusing on electric and autonomous vehicles. The company emphasizes innovation, sustainability, and advanced engineering, aiming to enhance user experience through smart connectivity and intelligent systems. Its organizational structure includes a significant number of ordinary shares, with a notable portion held by the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP and public shareholders. The company has undergone various mergers and acquisitions, including a reorganization to streamline operations and enhance control over its subsidiaries.

As of February 23, 2024, Lotus Technology's ordinary shares and Public Warrants began trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW." The company has issued multiple types of financial instruments, including Public Warrants, Sponsor Warrants, and exchangeable notes, which have been categorized based on their fair value measurements. The financial statements reflect a continuation of operations post-merger, treating the mergers as recapitalizations without recognizing goodwill or intangible assets.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

As of December 31, 2023, and 2022, the fair value of listed equity securities was US$3,326 and US$8,411, respectively.

[Table Level]  
- **Table Title**: Parent Company Condensed Statements of Comprehensive Loss  
- **Table Summary**: This table presents a detailed breakdown of Lotus Technology Inc.’s comprehensive loss, including operating expenses, interest income/expenses, investment results, foreign currency exchange impacts, and other income/loss items, for the years ended December 31, 2023, 2022, and 2021. The data is expressed in thousands of US dollars and reflects values prior to income tax impact as well as additional components contributing to comprehensive loss.  
- **Context**: This table is part of the financial statements of Lotus Technology Inc., providing insights into the entity’s standalone financial performance prepared under the equity method of accounting for investments in subsidiaries and VIEs. The context emphasizes there were no material contingencies, long-term obligations, or mandatory dividends outside of already-disclosed information.  
- **Special Notes**: All values are in thousands of US dollars (US$). Income taxes are assumed to be nil unless otherwise stated, and losses due to subsidiaries and consolidated VIEs are considered part of parent company losses.  
  
[Row Level]  
- **Row 1**: Total operating expenses for the year ended December 31 were $2,983 thousand in 2023, $11,127 thousand in 2022, and $263 thousand in 2021. The significant decrease in 2023 compared to 2022 suggests reduced operational spending.  
- **Row 2**: Interest income in 2023 was $6,979 thousand, significantly higher compared to $2,839 thousand in 2022. There was no data for 2021.  
- **Row 3**: Interest expenses for all three years (2023, 2022, and 2021) were not reported, indicated by dashes.  
- **Row 4**: Net investment loss was $5,084 thousand in 2023, $4,242 thousand in 2022, and not applicable in 2021. This shows a slight increase in losses over the two years.  
- **Row 5**: Foreign currency exchange losses were $171 thousand in 2023 and $13,068 thousand in 2022. In contrast, 2021 reflected a foreign currency exchange gain of $2,124 thousand, showcasing considerable volatility in exchange impacts across the years.  
- **Row 6**: Changes in the fair value of convertible notes excluding the impact of instrument-specific credit risk resulted in a loss of $616 thousand in 2023, with no comparable figures for 2022 and 2021.  
- **Row 7**: Changes in the fair value of put option liabilities led to a loss of $2,508 thousand in 2023. No values were reported for this in 2022 and 2021.  
- **Row 8**: The share of losses from subsidiaries and consolidated VIEs amounted to $737,618 thousand in 2023, compared to $698,323 thousand in 2022 and $112,392 thousand in 2021, reflecting a consistent increase in losses attributable to subsidiaries.  
- **Row 9**: Loss before income taxes was $742,001 thousand in 2023, $723,921 thousand in 2022, and $110,531 thousand in 2021. The substantial losses signify a widening negative financial performance trend.  
- **Row 10**: Income tax expense remained nil for all three years, indicating no tax obligations for Lotus Technology Inc. during this period.  
- **Row 11**: The net loss was identical to the loss before income taxes: $742,001 thousand in 2023, $723,921 thousand in 2022, and $110,531 thousand in 2021.  
  
Other Comprehensive Income (Loss):  
- **Row 12**: Fair value changes in convertible notes due to instrument-specific credit risk resulted in a loss of $8,650 thousand in 2023, $893 thousand in 2022, and a gain of $119 thousand in 2021, thus displaying a worsening trend over time.  
- **Row 13**: Foreign currency translation adjustments (net of nil income taxes) yielded a gain of $16,210 thousand in 2023, $18,669 thousand in 2022, and a loss of $843 thousand in 2021, showing improvements in recent years.  
  
- **Row 14**: Total comprehensive loss for Lotus Technology Inc. was $734,441 thousand in 2023, $706,145 thousand in 2022, and $111,255 thousand in 2021. The increase in losses underscores deteriorating financial health at the parent company level.

title: EXHIBIT B 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a forward-thinking company specializing in innovative technology solutions, including software development, data analytics, and digital transformation. The firm is dedicated to enhancing business efficiency and fostering growth for its clients through customized services and advanced technology. With a strong focus on research and development, Lotus aims to remain at the forefront of industry trends, delivering high-quality products that address the dynamic needs of businesses. The company prioritizes collaboration, customer satisfaction, and sustainability in its operations. However, it is important to note that the securities associated with Lotus Technology have not been registered under the Securities Act of 1933 or similar laws, and cannot be transferred or sold without an effective registration statement or applicable exemptions, in accordance with the Convertible Note Purchase Agreement with Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited dated June 24, 2024. Any unauthorized attempts to transfer or sell these securities will be deemed void.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The transactions contemplated by the Merger Agreement are expected to be completed in the first quarter of 2024, and would result in Lotus Technology Inc. becoming a public company. Upon completion of the business combination, the combined company is expected to retain Lotus Technology Inc.’s name as “Lotus Technology Inc.” and its American Depositary Shares (ADS) are expected to be listed on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “LOT.”

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Showcases
Autonomous Driving Progress at CES 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is set to showcase its advanced autonomous driving solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, highlighting its commitment to innovation and safety. The company's intelligent driving division, Lotus Robotics, will present proprietary software and hardware, including the ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy, which enhance driver assistance, enable remote operations, and manage cloud data. These technologies are integral to Lotus Tech's vehicles, such as the hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, which feature cutting-edge hardware like dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips and a unique cognition module for risk management.

Recently, Lotus Tech received approval to test Level 3 automated driving on highways in Wuxi, China, a pilot zone for vehicle connectivity. This allows Eletre drivers to utilize its L3 capabilities on designated highways, supported by the vehicle's advanced Level 4-ready hardware, including a deployable LIDAR system and multiple sensors for enhanced perception. Lotus Robotics is also promoting self-driving technology through its ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

As part of its Vision80 business transformation strategy, Lotus Tech is enhancing its global R&D capabilities, with CEO Mr. Qingfeng Feng expressing optimism about the future of intelligent driving. The company is also nearing the completion of a merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, expected to finalize in early 2024, which will transition Lotus Tech to a public entity listed on Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." Visitors can explore Lotus Robotics' innovations at booth 9217 in the Tech East AI pavilion from January 9-12.

summary: The document includes a signature from Sanford Litvack, indicating his approval, and a section for notices, although the specific address is not mentioned. It features a "Signature Page to Registration Rights Agreement," which formalizes the agreement between parties regarding registration rights, requiring signatures to confirm their consent to the terms. This page is essential for validating the agreement and ensuring legal compliance concerning the registration of securities. Anish Melwani is also noted as a signatory, although his address for notices is not provided. The document outlines the rights associated with Ordinary Shares and warrants of Lotus Technology Inc., which are traded on the Nasdaq Stock Market. The company's warrants are listed under the ticker symbol "LOTWW," and all capitalized terms are defined in the company's Memorandum and Articles of Association or the annual report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2023.

Each row details specific security types, including units, Class A Ordinary Shares, and redeemable warrants, along with their associated features and trading information.

These securities are tied to L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp's financial offerings as part of its acquisition activities.  
- **Special Notes**: All securities are traded on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC. Units consist of one Class A Ordinary Share and one-third of one redeemable warrant, exercisable at an exercise price of $11.50 for each Class A Ordinary Share.

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1**: Units consist of one Class A Ordinary Share with a $0.0001 par value and one-third of one redeemable warrant. These securities trade under the symbol "LCAAU" on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC.  
- **Row 2**: Class A Ordinary Shares, which are included as part of the units, trade individually under the symbol "LCAA" on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC.  
- **Row 3**: Redeemable warrants, included in the units, allow holders to exercise one whole warrant for one Class A Ordinary Share at an exercise price of $11.50. These warrants trade under the symbol "LCAAW" on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC.

metadata.title_summary:
title: UNITED STATES SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION 
summary: Form 8-K is a crucial report that publicly traded companies must file with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to disclose significant corporate events that could affect shareholders' interests. This report aims to promote transparency by informing investors about material developments such as mergers, executive changes, and financial restatements.

In a recent Form 8-K filing dated February 8, 2024, L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, a company incorporated in the Cayman Islands—a jurisdiction known for its favorable business environment and tax benefits—reported various corporate details. The filing includes the company's commission file number (001-40196) and its Employer Identification Number (EIN: 98-1577355), which are essential for regulatory identification and tax purposes.

The report also provides the company's principal executive office address in Singapore and lists its securities, which include units, Class A Ordinary Shares, and redeemable warrants, all traded on The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC. The filing indicates whether the company qualifies as an emerging growth company, a designation that allows it to benefit from reduced regulatory requirements, thus facilitating its growth and capital-raising efforts. Additionally, the report addresses compliance with various securities regulations and confirms the absence of solicitation materials related to the filing.

Overall, this Form 8-K serves to keep investors informed about L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp's significant corporate activities and compliance with regulatory standards, reinforcing the importance of transparency in the financial markets.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The board of directors of Lotus Technology will determine whether, to what extent, at what times and places, and under what conditions or regulations the accounts and books will be open to inspection by shareholders, and no shareholder will otherwise have any right to inspect any account, book, or document of Lotus Technology except as required by law or authorized by the board of directors or the shareholders by special resolution. Under the Delaware General Corporation Law, any shareholder of Lotus Technology may for any proper purpose inspect or make copies of Lotus Technology’s stock ledger, list of shareholders, and other books and records. Shareholders of Cayman Islands exempted companies like Lotus Technology have no general right under Cayman Islands law to inspect corporate records (other than the memorandum and articles of association, the register of mortgages and charges, and any special resolutions passed by Lotus Technology's shareholders) or obtain copies of the list of shareholders of these companies. Under Cayman Islands law, the names of Lotus Technology's current directors can be obtained from a search conducted at the Registrar of Companies. However, Lotus Technology intends to provide its shareholders with annual reports containing audited financial statements.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Inspection of Books and Records 
summary: The board of directors determines the conditions for shareholder access to the company's accounts and records, including the timing, extent, and location of inspections. Shareholders can only inspect documents if allowed by law, authorized by the board, or through a special resolution. Under Delaware General Corporation Law, shareholders have the right to inspect the stock ledger, shareholder list, and other relevant records for legitimate purposes. In contrast, shareholders of Cayman Islands exempted companies have limited rights to inspect corporate records, only accessing specific documents like the memorandum and articles of association, the register of mortgages and charges, and special resolutions. While the names of current directors are available through the Registrar of Companies, shareholders receive annual reports containing audited financial statements.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: LCAA’s securities, namely the Units (trading symbol “LCAAU”), LCAA Public Shares (trading symbol “LCAA”) and LCAA Public Warrants (trading symbol “LCAAW”), are currently listed on Nasdaq. The Units, LCAA Public Shares and LCAA Public Warrants will cease trading upon consummation of the Business Combination and will be delisted from Nasdaq and deregistered under the Exchange Act. Lotus Technology Corporation intends to apply for listing of the LTC ADSs on Nasdaq under the proposed symbol “LOT” and LTC Warrants under the proposed symbol “LOTWW,” each to be effective upon the consummation of the Business Combination. While trading on Nasdaq is expected to begin on the first business day following the consummation of the Business Combination, there can be no assurance that the LTC ADSs and LTC Warrants will be listed on Nasdaq or that a viable and active trading market will develop. See “Risk Factors” for more information.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Q:  What will happen to LCAA’s securities upon consummation of the Business Combination? 
summary: LCAA's securities, which include Units (LCAAU), Public Shares (LCAA), and Public Warrants (LCAAW), are currently traded on Nasdaq but will be delisted following the completion of the Business Combination. Subsequently, LTC intends to seek a listing for its American Depositary Shares (ADSs) under the symbol "LOT" and LTC Warrants under "LOTWW" on Nasdaq, expected to begin trading the day after the Business Combination is finalized. However, there is no assurance that the LTC ADSs and Warrants will be successfully listed or that a strong trading market will develop, with further information available in the "Risk Factors" section.
